mensHealth.tipSleepLow: Mindre end 7 timers søvn reducerer testosteron med op til 15%. Prioriter 8 timer.
mensHealth.tipStressHigh: Højt kortisol undertrykker testosteron direkte. Ashwagandha + meditation anbefales.
mensHealth.tipDriveLow: Lav libido er et tidligt tegn på suboptimalt testosteron eller forhøjet prolaktin.
mensHealth.tipRecoveryLow: Dårlig restitution øger kortisol og hæmmer testosteronsyntesen. Tjek søvn + zink.
mensHealth.tipFocusLow: Lav mental klarhed er knyttet til suboptimalt testosteron og højt kortisol.
mensHealth.tipActivity: Styrketræning 3-4 gange om ugen er mest effektivt for testosteron baseret på evidens.
mensHealth.phaseOffDesc: Naturlig hormonproduktion genopretter sig. Fokus på helsemarkører og naturlig optimering.
mensHealth.phaseBlastDesc: Maksimal anabol fase. Maksimer træningsstimulus, overvåg blodtryk og organmarkører.
mensHealth.phaseCruiseDesc: Vedligeholdelsesdosis. Bro mellem blasts. Overvåg hæmatokrit og lipider regelmæssigt.
mensHealth.phasePCTDesc: Post-cycle terapi. HPTA-genstart i gang. Prioriter restitution, søvn og helsemarkører.
mensHealth.quizHeroSub: 4 spørgsmål til at opbygge din protokol — fra naturlig testosteronoptimering til avanceret atletstyring. Alle data er private.
mensHealth.articleDisclaimer: Pædagogisk indhold, ikke medicinsk rådgivning. Konsulter altid en læge for personlig vejledning.
mensHealth.vitalitySub: Evaluer hver søjle ærligt. Din T-Opt score opdateres i realtid.
mensHealth.pelvicSub: Forbedrer erektil funktion, urinvejskontrol, seksuel ydeevne og kernestabilitet.
mensHealth.pelvicInstructions: Knib 5s → Slap af 5s → Gentag. 10–15 cyklusser. Daglig træning giver resultater på 4–6 uger.
mensHealth.unlockStackSub: Opgrader til Pro for at se personlige anbefalinger baseret på dine mål.
mensHealth.suppLocked: Personlig tilpasning låst for gratis brugere.
mensHealth.suppDisclaimer: Konsulter altid en læge før du starter på tilskud, især ved medicinering eller under cyklus.
mensHealth.healthMarkersSub: Log dine seneste laboratorieværdier og blodtryksmålinger. Følg tendenser over tid.
mensHealth.peakDay7Body: Tøm glykogen. Kulhydrater: 1,5g/kg. Moderat natrium. Normalt vandindtag.
mensHealth.peakDay6Body: Lavkarbo (1g/kg). Højt proteinindhold. Begynd at overvåge daglige væktsvingninger.
mensHealth.peakDay5Body: Bemærk muskel-fylde og vaskularitet. Øg kulhydrater hvis du ser flad ud.
mensHealth.peakDay4Body: Kulhydrater: 4–6g/kg. Kilder med lavt GI: sød kartoffel, ris, havre. Hold fedtindtaget lavt.
mensHealth.peakDay3Body: Kulhydrater: 6–8g/kg. Hold øje med spill-over (udvisket definition). Reducer om nødvendigt.
mensHealth.peakDay2Body: Reducer vandindtaget en smule (ikke fjern). Øg kaliumrige fødevarer. Sidste natriumjustering.
mensHealth.peakDay1Body: Nip til vandet. Kulhydratlad backstage (riskager + honning). Pump op 20 min før scenen. Stol på din prep.
mensHealth.emptyTrack: Log din vitalitet dagligt for at se din tendens her.
mensHealth.peakWeekSub: Kulhydratladning dag for dag, vandstyring og sceneforberedelse
mensHealth.peakWeekDetailedSub: Carb load day-by-day, water manipulation and stage guidance
mensHealth.annualPanelBody: Som {{age}}-årig giver årlig sporing af testosteron, SHBG, østradiol og stofskifte dig data til proaktiv optimering.
mensHealth.fatLossBody: Aggressiv kalorierestriktion under 15 kcal/kg/dag undertrykker testosteron. Sigt efter max 15–20% underskud. Prioriter protein 2,2g/kg for at bevare muskler.
mensHealth.progressiveBody: Tilføj 2,5 kg til baseløft ugentligt, eller øg reps før du øger vægten. Træn hver muskelgruppe 2x om ugen.
mensHealth.unlockDeepSub: AI hormonanalyse, træningsanbefalinger og protokoller for forstærkede atleter.

vitD.name: D3-vitamin + K2
vitD.reason: Vigtigt for testosteronsyntese; 80% af mænd har mangel

mag.name: Magnesiumbisglycinat
mag.reason: Øger frit testosteron med op til 24%; forbedrer djupsøvn

zinc.name: Zink (Bisglycinat)
zinc.reason: Vigtigt mineral for testosteronproduktion og sædkvalitet

ashwa.name: Ashwagandha KSM-66
ashwa.reason: Reducerer kortisol med 30%; øger testosteron ved stress

creatine.name: Kreatin monohydrat
creatine.reason: Mest evidensbaserede tilskud; øger styrke, muskler og kognition

carnitine.name: L-Carnitin L-Tartrat
carnitine.reason: Øger tætheden af androgenreceptorer; støtter fedtforbrænding

tongkat.name: Tongkat Ali (200:1 ekstrakt)
tongkat.reason: Øger frit testosteron ved at sænke SHBG; bevist for mænd over 35

coq10.name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)
coq10.reason: Støtter mitokondrierne; vigtigt for energi og hjertet over 35

apigenin.name: Apigenin
apigenin.reason: Naturligt beroligende; forbedrer indsovning uden afhængighed

tudca.name: TUDCA (Galdesyre)
tudca.reason: KRITISK: Leverbeskyttende middel; beskytter leveren ved brug af orale præparater

nac.name: NAC (N-acetylcystein)
nac.reason: Lever- & hjertebeskyttelse; reducerer oxidativ stress fra androgener

hawthorn.name: Hvidtjørn bær
hawthorn.reason: Kredsløbsstøtte; hjælper med at håndtere forhøjet blodtryk

celery.name: Sellerifrøekstrakt
celery.reason: Naturlig aromatasemodulator; hjælper med at styre østrogen

clomiphene.name: Klomifen / Enclomifene
clomiphene.reason: HPTA-genstart; stimulerer LH/FSH — rådfør dig med læge

hcg.name: HCG (hvis anvendt)
hcg.reason: Opretholder testikelvolumen og funktion under cyklus

enzymes.name: Fordøjelsesenzymer
enzymes.reason: Nødvendigt ved meget højt proteinindtag for at undgå mavegener

art1.title: Testosteron-ritningen
art1.body: Testosteron topper mellem kl. 06–08 og er lavest om natten. Søvn er den vigtigste faktor — én uge med 5 timers søvn reducerer testosteron til kastreret niveau hos unge mænd. Protokollen: 8 timers søvn, styrketræning 4x/uge, zink 30mg, D3-vitamin 5000IU og kortisolstyring.

art2.title: Søvn og testosteron: Den hårde videnskab
art2.body: 95% af den daglige testosteronproduktion sker under REM-søvnen. Hver time under 8 reducerer produktionen proportionelt. Alkohol om aftenen hæmmer GH-frigivelse med 70%.

art3.title: Træning for maksimal testosteronrespons
art3.body: Baseløft (squat, dødløft, bænkpres, roning) med høj intensitet (75–90% 1RM) giver den største respons. Hold sessionerne under 60 minutter — kortisol stiger stejlt derefter.

art4.title: Kost for testosteron
art4.body: Fedt er råmaterialet til testosteron — at gå under 20% fedt i kosten ødelægger niveauerne. Kolesterol fra æg og rødt kød er forløberen. Undgå kronisk kalorierestriktion.

art5.title: Kortisol: Testosteronets fjende
art5.body: Kortisol og testosteron er direkte modstandere. Hver kortisoltop hæmmer testosteronsyntesen. De mest effektive værktøjer: konsekvent søvn, ashwagandha og begrænsning af koffein efter kl. 12.

art6.title: Skadesreduktion for PED-brugere
art6.body: Største risiko er hjerte-kar-sygdomme. Blodprøver hver 8. uge (lipider, CBC, leverværdier) og konditionstræning 4x/uge er obligatorisk for organbeskyttelse.

art7.title: Peak Week: Videnskaben om at komme ind tør
art7.body: Peak week handler om glykogensuperkompensation, ikke ekstrem udtørring. Kulhydratladning i de sidste 3 dage. Vand- og natriummanipulering de sidste 24 timer.

art8.title: Hvilke blodprøver alle mænd bør følge
art8.body: Basispanelet: Totalt og frit testosteron, SHBG, LH, FSH, østradiol, lipider, lever/nyre-værdier, D-vitamin og HbA1c. Test dig altid om morgenen i fastende tilstand.

bp.label: Blodtryk
bp.tip: Tjek ugentligt under cyklus. Hvidtjørn + sellerifrø hvis forhøjet.

hct.label: Hæmatokrit
hct.tip: Forhøjet HCT = risiko for blodpropper. Giv blod eller reducer dosis.

ldl.label: LDL Kolesterol
ldl.tip: Androgener kan forværre lipidprofilen. Fokuser på omega-3 og kardio.

liver.label: ALT (Lever)
liver.tip: Vigtigt ved brug af orale præparater. TUDCA + NAC er obligatorisk.

psa.label: PSA (40+)
psa.tip: Prostatahelbred. Årligt tjek for alle forstærkede brugere over 40 år.

enhanced.bloodworkBody: Enhanced athletes must monitor lipids, liver enzymes, haematocrit, and hormones every 8 weeks minimum. These values change rapidly with AAS use. Get bloods before, during and after each cycle.
enhanced.cardioBody: The #1 cause of premature death in enhanced athletes is cardiovascular disease from LV hypertrophy, elevated haematocrit and dyslipidaemia. 4x/week cardio (zone 2) is non-negotiable, not optional.

quiz.heroSub: 4 hurtige spørgsmål. Vi skræddersyr dine opgaver og AI-tips til din arbejdsstil.
quiz.knowTitle: ✦ Vi ved allerede dette om dig
quiz.q4: Hvad er den ENE ting, du skal levere i denne uge?
quiz.q4Placeholder: f.eks. Lukke aftalen, sende v1, løbe 5 km…
quiz.opt_procrastination: Komme i gang med svære ting

tips.focus_1: Begynd med din sværeste opgave før du tjekker e-mail.
tips.focus_2: Aktiver Focus Mode for en 25-minutters distraktionsfri spurt.
tips.focus_3: Skriv din vigtigste opgave her før alt andet.
tips.overload_1: Spis frøen: identificer den ENE opgave, der betyder mest.
tips.overload_2: Saml lignende opgaver — alle opkald sammen, alt skrivearbejde sammen.
tips.overload_3: Sig nej til én ting i dag. Læg det i noter, ikke på listen.
tips.procrastination_1: Start med en 2-minutters version af den skræmmende opgave.
tips.procrastination_2: Kobl opgaven til musik eller en belønning bagefter.
tips.procrastination_3: Fortæl en partner om dit mål — ansvarlighed virker.
tips.balance_1: Book personlig tid som faste hændelser i kalenderen.
tips.balance_2: Log din aftenrutine i Velvære → Søvntracker.
tips.balance_3: Sæt en fast sluttid for dagen og overhold den.

fallbackSessions.quickResetDesc: En kort pause til at finde midten under en travl dag.
fallbackSessions.anxietyReleaseDesc: Giv slip på spændinger og find din indre ro.

mentalHealth.heroSubFree: Et 28-dages evidensbaseret program. Gratis brugere får en fuld plan. Opgrader til Pro for at fortsætte.
mentalHealth.pillarSub: Aktiviteter fuldført inden for hver dimension

mentalHealth.disclaimer: Dette program er baseret på evidens fra KBT, ACT og neurovidenskab. Erstat ikke professionel hjælp.

themes.w4Tagline: Gør det til den du er

days.d1Affirm: Jeg er præcis, hvor jeg skal være.
days.d1Reflect: Hvad giver dig ro lige nu?
days.d2Affirm: I listen to what my body is telling me.
days.d2Reflect: Where do you carry tension today? Why might that be?
days.d3Affirm: I give and receive support freely.
days.d3Reflect: Who in your life lifts you up? When did you last tell them?
days.d4Affirm: Rest is productive. I honour my need for stillness.
days.d4Reflect: What drains your energy? What restores it?
days.d5Affirm: My voice matters. I express myself with confidence.
days.d5Reflect: What would you say if fear were not in the way?
days.d6Affirm: I live in alignment with what matters most to me.
days.d6Reflect: Name your top 3 values. Did your week reflect them?
days.d7Affirm: I acknowledge every step I take toward my wellbeing.
days.d7Reflect: What was your biggest win this week — however small?
days.d8Reflect: What stresses you most right now? Is it urgent, important, both, or neither?
days.d9Affirm: I choose how I interpret my world.
days.d9Reflect: Take a stressful thought. What is another way to see it?
days.d10Reflect: What emotion have you been avoiding? What might it be trying to tell you?
days.d11Affirm: My "no" is an act of self-respect.
days.d11Reflect: Where in your life do you need a firmer boundary?
days.d12Affirm: I treat myself with the kindness I give others.
days.d12Reflect: What would you say to a friend going through what you are facing?
days.d13Affirm: I protect my energy without guilt.
days.d13Reflect: Which relationships give you energy? Which deplete it?
days.d14Affirm: Growth takes time. I am patient with myself.
days.d14Reflect: How have you grown since Day 1?
days.d15Reflect: What topic have you always wanted to learn but kept putting off?
days.d16Affirm: I walk toward what scares me — one step at a time.
days.d16Reflect: What is one small thing you have been avoiding due to fear?
days.d17Affirm: When I lose track of time, I am most alive.
days.d17Reflect: When were you last completely absorbed in something? What were you doing?
days.d18Affirm: My life is meaningful because I choose to make it so.
days.d18Reflect: What would make your life feel more meaningful?
days.d19Affirm: My strengths are unique gifts I offer the world.
days.d19Reflect: What are you naturally good at that others often notice?
days.d20Affirm: Forgiveness is a gift I give myself.
days.d20Reflect: Who or what are you holding resentment toward? What would it feel like to release it?
days.d21Affirm: I am not who I was. I am becoming who I want to be.
days.d21Reflect: What surprised you about yourself in Week 3?
days.d22Affirm: My routines are the architecture of my best self.
days.d22Reflect: Which 3 practices from this programme do you want to keep forever?
days.d23Affirm: I am the person I have chosen to become.
days.d23Reflect: Write your new identity statement: "I am someone who ____."
days.d24Affirm: I am surrounded by people who want me to thrive.
days.d24Reflect: Who in your life supports your mental health journey?
days.d25Affirm: My future self is worth investing in today.
days.d25Reflect: Write a letter from your future self one year from now.
days.d26Affirm: I have the tools. I choose to use them.
days.d26Reflect: What will you do when things get hard again?
days.d27Affirm: I am proud of the work I have done.
days.d27Reflect: What are you most proud of about this month?
days.d28Affirm: Hver dag er en ny begyndelse.
days.d28Reflect: Hvordan ser det næste kapitel ud?

activities.d1a1Desc: Nævn 5 ting du ser, 4 du kan røre ved, 3 du hører, 2 du lugter, 1 du smager.
activities.d1a3Desc: Skriv 3 specifikke ting ned fra i dag.
activities.d1a3Why: Gratitude activates the medial prefrontal cortex, reducing anxiety.
activities.d2a1Desc: Tense each muscle group for 5s, release for 30s. Start at feet, end at face.
activities.d2a1Why: Reduces physical tension that mirrors mental stress.
activities.d2a2Desc: Write 3 pages of stream-of-consciousness immediately after waking. No editing.
activities.d2a2Why: Clears mental "noise" and surfaces unconscious concerns.
activities.d3a1Desc: Text or call someone you appreciate. No agenda. Just warmth.
activities.d3a1Why: Acts of kindness release oxytocin, reducing cortisol for both parties.
activities.d3a2Desc: Silently wish wellbeing to yourself → a loved one → a neutral person → a difficult person.
activities.d3a2Why: Increases positive emotion and social connectedness (Fredrickson, 2008).
activities.d4a1Desc: Lie down. Follow a Non-Sleep Deep Rest protocol. Stay awake but fully relaxed.
activities.d4a1Why: Restores dopamine levels comparable to a 90-minute nap (Huberman Lab, 2021).
activities.d4a2Desc: No screens 1 hour before bed. Replace with reading, stretching, or journaling.
activities.d4a2Why: Blue light delays melatonin by up to 3 hours. Sleep quality rises 34% without it.
activities.d5a1Desc: Look into your eyes in a mirror. Say 3 affirmations aloud. Notice discomfort without judgement.
activities.d5a2Desc: Draw, write, sing, dance — anything creative. No goal. No audience. Just expression.
activities.d5a2Why: Art-making reduces cortisol by up to 75% in 45 minutes (Stuckey & Nobel, 2010).
activities.d5a3Desc: End your shower on cold for 2 minutes. Breathe slowly through the discomfort.
activities.d6a1Desc: List 10 things you value. Rank them. Write one action this week that honoured each top 3.
activities.d6a1Why: Values clarification is a core ACT technique reducing psychological inflexibility.
activities.d6a2Desc: Lie down. Slowly bring awareness to each body part from feet to crown.
activities.d6a2Why: Reduces insomnia severity by 58% (Ong et al., 2014).
activities.d7a1Desc: Write: 3 wins, 1 challenge, 1 lesson, and 1 intention for next week.
activities.d7a1Why: Reflective journaling consolidates neurological learning patterns.
activities.d7a2Desc: Do one thing purely for joy — a film, a meal, a walk with someone you love.
activities.d7a2Why: Reward prediction reinforces habit formation (Deci & Ryan, Self-Determination Theory).
activities.d8a1Desc: Draw a circle. Inside = things you control. Outside = things you cannot. Move your worries.
activities.d8a2Desc: Wish → Outcome → Obstacle → Plan. Write one WOOP for the week.
activities.d8a2Why: WOOP nearly doubles goal achievement vs. positive thinking alone (Oettingen, 2014).
activities.d9a1Desc: Take your most stressful thought. Write: Evidence for → Evidence against → Alternative view.
activities.d9a1Why: Core CBT technique. Reduces depressive symptoms by up to 40% (Beck, 2011).
activities.d9a2Why: Activates the vagus nerve, shifting the nervous system into rest-and-digest.
activities.d10a1Desc: Use an emotion wheel to name your feelings precisely. Go beyond "bad" or "sad".
activities.d10a1Why: Labelling emotions reduces amygdala activation immediately (Lieberman, 2007).
activities.d10a2Desc: Any movement you enjoy. Focus on how your body feels, not performance.
activities.d10a2Why: Exercise is as effective as antidepressants for mild-moderate depression (Blumenthal, 2007).
activities.d11a1Desc: Write one boundary you need to set this week. Practice saying it aloud 3 times.
activities.d11a1Why: Assertiveness training reduces social anxiety and improves relationship satisfaction.
activities.d11a2Desc: Lie down. Follow a body scan or Yoga Nidra audio.
activities.d11a2Why: Restores alertness without caffeine; improves afternoon cognitive performance 20%.
activities.d12a1Desc: Write a letter to yourself about a current struggle — as if writing to your best friend.
activities.d12a1Why: Self-compassion writing reduces shame and self-criticism (Neff & Germer, 2013).
activities.d12a2Desc: Recall a positive memory in vivid detail for 10 minutes. Write what you sensed.
activities.d12a2Why: Savouring boosts positive affect and extends the benefits of good experiences.
activities.d13a1Desc: List all people/activities from the past week. Rate each +/- on your energy.
activities.d13a1Why: Conscious energy management prevents burnout (Loehr & Schwartz, 2003).
activities.d13a2Desc: One hour with all notifications off. Be entirely present with one analogue activity.
activities.d13a2Why: Phone-free periods reduce anxiety scores by 23% (Duke University, 2021).
activities.d14a1Desc: Wins → Challenges → Patterns noticed → One commitment for Week 3.
activities.d14a1Why: Structured reflection embeds learning and prepares the brain for next-phase change.
activities.d15a1Desc: Pick one topic. Read, watch, or listen with no distraction for 30 minutes.
activities.d15a1Why: Neuroplasticity peaks with focused, novel learning — 30 min is optimal for retention.
activities.d15a2Desc: Explain what you just learned in simple words, as if to a 10-year-old.
activities.d15a2Why: The Feynman Technique reveals gaps in understanding and deepens memory encoding.
activities.d16a1Desc: Identify your fear. Break it into 10 steps from easiest to hardest. Take step 1 today.
activities.d16a1Why: Gradual exposure systematically reduces avoidance and anxiety responses (CBT).
activities.d17a1Desc: Do one activity that makes you lose track of time. Remove all distractions.
activities.d17a1Why: Flow state activates dopamine, norepinephrine, and endorphins simultaneously.
activities.d18a1Desc: Draw the Ikigai diagram: What you love, are good at, the world needs, can be paid for.
activities.d18a1Why: Ikigai alignment predicts life satisfaction and longevity (Okinawa Blue Zone research).
activities.d19a1Desc: List your top 5 character strengths. Write one way you used each this week.
activities.d19a1Why: Using top strengths daily reduces depression by 40% (Seligman, PERMA model).
activities.d20a1Desc: Write a letter of forgiveness — you do not have to send it. Focus on your own release.
activities.d20a1Why: Forgiveness reduces hostility, blood pressure, and depression scores significantly.
activities.d21a1Desc: Re-read your Day 1 entry. Write: Who you were → Who you are now. Celebrate the difference.
activities.d21a1Why: Completion rituals anchor gains and motivate continued behaviour change.
activities.d22a1Desc: Write 3 habits: Trigger → New behaviour → Reward. Stack them onto existing routines.
activities.d22a1Why: Habit stacking has a 91% success rate vs. solo habit formation (Fogg, BJ, 2020).
activities.d23a1Desc: Write 5 "I am" statements that describe who you are becoming. Read them daily.
activities.d23a1Why: Identity-based habits are 2× more durable than outcome-based goals (Clear, Atomic Habits).
activities.d24a1Desc: Draw your support map: emotional, practical, professional, and social contacts.
activities.d24a1Why: Social support is the strongest predictor of resilience (Southwick & Charney, 2012).
activities.d25a1Desc: Write from your future self (12 months ahead). What did you do? Who are you?
activities.d25a1Why: Future self visualisation increases present self-regulation by 30% (Hershfield, 2011).
activities.d26a1Desc: Write your mental health crisis plan: warning signs, triggers, interventions, people to call.
activities.d26a1Why: Having a written plan reduces the duration and impact of mental health crises.
activities.d26a2Desc: Roleplay a hard day mentally. What do you do first? Second? Who do you call?
activities.d26a2Why: Mental rehearsal primes neural pathways to execute coping responses automatically.
activities.d27a1Desc: Write thank-you notes to people who supported you this month. Send at least one.
activities.d27a1Why: Expressed gratitude deepens relationships and increases personal wellbeing scores.
activities.d28a1Desc: Re-read your Day 1 entry. Write: Who you were → Who you are → Who you are becoming. Set Month 2 intentions.
activities.d28a1Why: Narrative continuity supports identity integration and sustained behaviour change.

profile.title: Profil

phaseDesc.menstrual: The uterine lining sheds. Oestrogen and progesterone are at their lowest.
phaseDesc.follicular: FSH rises, follicles develop, oestrogen climbs. Energy and clarity increase.
phaseDesc.ovulation: LH surge triggers egg release. Oestrogen peaks. You feel your best.
phaseDesc.lutealEarly: Progesterone rises with corpus luteum formation. Body temperature elevates.
phaseDesc.lutealLate: PMS window. Progesterone drops if no fertilisation. Cortisol sensitivity rises.

phaseNutrition.menstrual: Iron-rich foods: lentils, leafy greens, red meat
phaseNutrition.follicular: Complex carbs, fermented foods, lean protein
phaseNutrition.lutealEarly: Magnesium (dark chocolate, nuts), B6 for mood
phaseNutrition.lutealLate: Reduce salt + sugar. Tryptophan foods (turkey, eggs) for serotonin.

phaseTip.menstrual: Use a heat pad, cut caffeine and prioritise sleep.
phaseTip.follicular: Schedule demanding projects and creative work now — your brain is in turbo mode.
phaseTip.ovulation: Your voice is literally more attractive now — lean into social situations.
phaseTip.lutealEarly: This is the best phase for detailed admin work and deep focus.
phaseTip.lutealLate: Reduce alcohol and processed foods. Track your PMS symptoms to identify patterns.

insight.pmsBody: Magnesium glycinate (300mg) taken before bed has strong clinical evidence for reducing cramps, bloating and mood changes.
insight.stressLutealBody: Your stress level combined with the PMS window can amplify cortisol and worsen symptoms. Prioritise a cortisol-reduction practice today.
insight.fertilityBody: You're in your peak fertility window. If you're actively trying to conceive, this is the optimal time. If not, stay aware.
insight.estrogenBody: Serotonin and dopamine are elevated in this phase. A great window to schedule difficult conversations, presentations or creative projects.
insight.sleepBody: Under 7 hours of sleep raises cortisol, suppresses progesterone and can cause cycle irregularities. You are currently below the minimum.
insight.lowCarbBody: Very low carb diets can suppress LH and FSH, causing irregular or absent periods. If your cycle is irregular, consider a carb-cycling approach.
insight.painBody: Severe menstrual pain is not normal and may indicate endometriosis, fibroids or adenomyosis. We strongly recommend speaking to a doctor or gynaecologist.
insight.pillBody: The combined pill can deplete B6, B12, folate, magnesium and zinc. Consider a B-complex supplement and regular testing.
insight.athleticBody: High-intensity training can suppress oestrogen and disrupt your cycle (RED-S). Monitor your period — any disruption is a warning signal from your body.
insight.periBody: Hormonal fluctuations often begin in the late 30s, even with regular periods. Track mood, sleep and cycle changes closely.
insight.calorieBody: Aggressive caloric deficits suppress leptin and thyroid hormones, potentially stalling weight loss and disrupting cycles. Sync training intensity with your cycle.

magnesium.name: Magnesium Glycinate
magnesium.reason: Reduces cramps, PMS mood symptoms and improves sleep

iron.name: Iron + Vitamin C
iron.reason: Replenish iron lost during menstruation; C enhances absorption

omega3.name: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)
omega3.reason: Anti-inflammatory; reduces prostaglandin-driven cramps. As effective as ibuprofen in trials

bComplex.name: B-Complex (B6, B12, Folate)
bComplex.reason: Oral contraceptives deplete B vitamins — critical for mood and methylation

zinc.name: Zinc
zinc.reason: Contraceptive pill depletes zinc, affecting immunity and skin

ashwagandha.name: Ashwagandha KSM-66
ashwagandha.reason: Clinically proven adaptogen for cortisol reduction and hormonal balance

magLThreonate.name: Magnesium L-Threonate
magLThreonate.reason: Crosses blood-brain barrier; improves deep sleep and reduces night anxiety

questions.goalLoseWeightInfo: Create a sustainable caloric deficit to lose fat while maintaining energy.
questions.goalBuildMuscleInfo: Maximize hypertrophy with progressive overload and optimal protein intake.
questions.goalMaintainInfo: Focus on performance and metabolic health without changing weight.
questions.goalEnduranceInfo: Optimize aerobic capacity, stamina, and cardiovascular efficiency.
questions.goalGeneralHealthInfo: Prioritize longevity, balanced energy, and disease prevention.
questions.goalBodyRecompInfo: Lose fat and gain muscle simultaneously. Ideal for reshaping the physique without drastic weight changes.
questions.wsStrengthInfo: Focus on building muscle and power through resistance.
questions.wsCardioInfo: Endurance activities like running, cycling, or swimming.
questions.wsHiitInfo: High-intensity interval training to burn calories quickly.
questions.wsYogaInfo: Focus on flexibility, balance, and mind-body connection.
questions.milestone: Main milestone for the next 12 months
questions.milestonePlaceholder: e.g., Run a marathon, buy a house...
questions.commitToast: Perfect! We will tailor your plan to your commitment level.
questions.commitEasyInfo: Gentle and sustainable changes at your own pace. Slower progress but easier to maintain.
questions.commitBalancedInfo: A steady and proven approach. Moderate changes with consistent results.
questions.commitMaximumInfo: All in for a rapid transformation. Requires intensive commitment but yields faster results.
questions.dietHighProteinInfo: Boosts metabolism and muscle recovery. Ideal for lean mass and appetite control.
questions.dietLowCarbInfo: Minimizes carbs to stabilize blood sugar and activate fat burning.
questions.dietBalancedInfo: A sustainable mix of all macronutrients.
questions.dietPlantBasedInfo: Rich in vegetables, fruits, legumes, and whole grains.
questions.dietFlexibleInfo: Track macros instead of restricting foods. Eat what you like within your goals.
